svg.font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, Arial, sans-serif
svg.text: The softmax makes P an exponential, and int8 has no exponent
Left: where a Qwen3.5-9B attention row keeps its probability mass, and which values each 8-bit grid can actually represent.
Right: per-channel absolute max of K, relative to the median channel. Measured on ZINC, layer 20, all 32 query heads, RX 9070 XT.
Where the attention mass lives, against the 8-bit grids
int8 rounds everything left of the line to zero
12 percent of the row mass at 4k. 38 percent at 32k.
share of the softmax row sum, by magnitude decade
127 uniform steps. The left three decades of the axis contain no representable value at all.
8 steps per binade, 18 binades with subnormals. Smallest representable probability about 4e-6.
Same 8 bits, same matrix rate. Only one of them spends the bits where the mass is.
A per-tensor int8 scale set by a 44x
so the subtraction is exact and free.
RDNA4 matrix core rates: 1024 FP16 FLOP and 2048 INT8 ops per clock per compute unit (AMD GPUOpen). P gets its quantization scale for free from the row
max that flash attention already tracks. What it does not get for free is a grid that can hold the tail of an exponential.
